title: butea varians
intro: Butea varians, also known as the dhak or palas tree, is a deciduous tree native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia, characterized by its fragrant flowers and medicinal properties. The plant is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Butea varians include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include tea, powder, and oil, which can be consumed or applied topically to reap its medicinal benefits.
